identity.name: District
identity.definition: A District is the top-level organizational entity representing a school district, diocese, charter network, or other educational authority that contracts with Renaissance for product licensing — serving as the parent container for all schools, users, data, and administrative policies across Renaissance products.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a District appears in grids and dashboards

attributes.name: District Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. The official name of the school district or educational organization.

attributes.name: State
attributes.description: US state or territory where the district is located.

attributes.name: School Count
attributes.description: Total number of schools within the district.

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Total number of active students across all schools in the district.

attributes.name: District Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. The official name of the school district or educational organization.

attributes.name: State
attributes.description: US state or territory where the district is located.

attributes.name: School Count
attributes.description: Total number of schools within the district.

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Total number of active students across all schools in the district.

attributes.name: Product Licenses
attributes.description: Which Renaissance products the district has licensed.

attributes.name: District Admin(s)
attributes.description: Administrators with district-level access and permissions.

attributes.name: NCES District ID
attributes.description: National Center for Education Statistics identifier for federal reporting and cross-system matching.

attributes.name: SIS Configuration
attributes.description: Configured Student Information System integrations (Clever, ClassLink, OneRoster).

attributes.name: Address
attributes.description: Physical mailing address of the district office.

attributes.name: Phone
attributes.description: Primary phone number for the district office.

attributes.name: Time Zone
attributes.description: Time zone for scheduling, reporting, and data sync windows.

attributes.name: District Type
attributes.description: Classification: Public, Private, Charter, or Diocese.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle state: Active or Inactive.

attributes.name: Contract Period
attributes.description: Start and end dates of the current Renaissance product license agreement.

stories.title: View District Dashboard
stories.benefit: I can see aggregate performance data, school counts, student counts, and product usage across my entire district.
stories.whenClause: I log into my district admin dashboard,
stories.thenClause: I see a summary of all schools, total student enrollment, licensed products, and key performance indicators for the current term.

stories.title: Manage District Schools
stories.benefit: I can add, remove, or configure schools within my district so each school has the correct products, SIS integration, and admin assignments.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to my district's school management area,
stories.thenClause: I see a list of all schools with their status, student counts, and product licenses, and I can add new schools or modify existing ones.

stories.title: Compare School Performance
stories.benefit: I can identify which schools are excelling and which need additional support by comparing assessment outcomes across the district.
stories.thenClause: I see comparative data across all schools — including Star scores, growth metrics, and product utilization rates — filterable by grade, subject, and time period.

businessRules.title: Top-Level Entity
businessRules.description: A District is the highest organizational container in the Renaissance hierarchy. All Schools, Users, and data exist within the scope of a District.

businessRules.title: Licensing Authority
businessRules.description: Product licenses are contracted at the District level. Individual schools inherit their product access from the district's license agreement.

businessRules.title: Admin Hierarchy
businessRules.description: District Admins have administrative access to all schools within their district. School-level admins cannot see or modify other schools' data.

businessRules.title: Data Aggregation
businessRules.description: District-level reports aggregate data across all schools. Student counts, assessment scores, and usage metrics roll up from School to District.

businessRules.title: SIS Integration
businessRules.description: SIS configuration (Clever, ClassLink, OneRoster) is set at the District level. All schools within the district share the same SIS pipeline and sync schedule.

businessRules.title: Policy Cascade
businessRules.description: District-level policies (assessment windows, product configurations, grading scales) cascade down to all schools unless overridden at the school level.

businessRules.title: Deactivation
businessRules.description: When a District is deactivated, all schools, users, and data within it become inaccessible. Historical data is retained for contractual and reporting purposes but no new activity can occur.

states.name: Provisioned
states.description: District record created, contract signed, awaiting initial setup

states.name: Active
states.description: District is fully operational with licensed products and active schools
states.triggers: Initial setup complete, schools and users configured

states.name: Inactive
states.description: Contract expired or district deactivated — data retained, access suspended

states.name: Reactivated
states.description: Previously inactive district restored with new or renewed contract
states.triggers: Contract renewal, re-provisioning by Renaissance Admin

relationships.mechanics: Schools are created within a District during provisioning or added later by District Admins. Each school inherits the district's product licenses and SIS configuration.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A District contains one or more Schools; a School belongs to exactly one District. Typical: 1–200 schools per district.
relationships.sorts: Schools within a District: alphabetical by name (default), by student count, by grade levels served.
relationships.filters: By grade band (elementary, middle, high), by product usage, by school type, by status (active/inactive).
relationships.dependencies: If a District is deactivated, all its Schools are also deactivated. Schools cannot exist without a parent District.

relationships.mechanics: Students belong to a District indirectly through their School enrollment. District-level aggregation sums all students across all schools.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many (indirect via School). A District has many Students through its Schools; a Student belongs to one District through their School. Typical: 500–100,000 students per district.
relationships.sorts: Students within a District: by school (default), by grade level, by last name.
relationships.filters: By school, by grade level, by enrollment status, by assessment performance, by demographic group.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student transfers schools within the same District, their data remains within the district scope. Cross-district transfers require separate handling.

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: All students enrolled across district schools

nestedObjects.name: Teacher
nestedObjects.description: All teachers employed across district schools

structure.evidence: A District has a rich set of attributes: name, state, school count, student count, product licenses, NCES ID, address, phone, time zone, district type, status, contract period, SIS configuration, and admin users. You can design a detail page for a District.

instances.evidence: Springfield Public Schools (IL, 25 schools, 15K students), Success Academy Charter Network (NY, 47 schools, 20K students), Diocese of Arlington Catholic Schools (VA, 38 schools, 12K students). Renaissance serves thousands of District instances.

purpose.evidence: Districts are the contracting entity — the reason Renaissance has customers. District Admins configure products, manage schools, view aggregated reports, and govern policies. All product licensing flows through District.

sipValidation.verdict: Core system object. The top-level organizational container and contracting entity.

synonyms.notes: Local Educational Agency — the official government term for a school district
Generic container term used in some internal tools
Charter networks like Success Academy or KIPP use this term
A diocese functions as a district for Catholic schools
Salesforce and billing systems refer to the contracting entity as Account

objectViews.description: Districts listed in an internal admin table with location, enrollment, and licensing data. Renaissance staff manage accounts and track customer scale from here.
The complete district record — schools, licensing, SIS configuration, and admin contacts. District admins manage their organization and access district-wide reports from this page.
A minimal row for selecting a district during setup or filtering. Shows district name, state, and school count — just enough to choose the right one.
The district displayed as a banner at the top of a report, providing organizational context for the data below. No interactive elements.
A summary card showing district size and location on a regional dashboard. Used to scan district-level metrics at a glance.
Districts matching a search query with state, school count, and student count. Used to find and navigate to a specific district.
